my_thoughts: They defined 'normal' as social consensus—'set by each society', 'ordinary man wants to be with others.' They're projecting intellectual framework onto social reality. I'm rejecting the category entirely: 'normal' and 'extraordinary' are both social constructs. 'Sanity' is just consensus hallucination. They'll ask for my definition ('Can I put this question back to you?')—that's the trap. I don't have one.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student defined 'normal' as social consensus ('set by each society', 'ordinary man wants to be with others'). They're projecting intellectual framework onto social reality. I'm rejecting the category: 'normal' and 'extraordinary' are both constructs. 'Sanity' is consensus hallucination. They'll ask for my definition.
visible_response:
Even such a man -- the 'extraordinary' man, as opposed to your 'ordinary' man -- has to live here in this society; he cannot run away and live in a cave and meditate. He is not in conflict with this society at all; he accepts the reality of the world... You are not separate from the society; you are 
the society.
